ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Venantius and Italica.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to the lord Venantius, Patrician, and Italica his wife.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I have taken care, with due affection, to enquire of certain persons who have come from Sicily about your Excellency’s health.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But they have given me a sad report of the frequency of your ailments.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now, when I say this, neither do I find anything to tell you about myself, except that, for my sins, lo it is now eleven months since it has been a very rare case with me if I have been able now and then to rise from my bed.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For I am afflicted by so great sufferings from gout, and so great from troubles, that my life is to me most grievous pain.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For every day I faint under my sufferings, and sigh in expectation of the relief of death.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Indeed among the clergy and people of this city there has been such an invasion of feverous sicknesses that hardly any freeman, hardly any slave, remains fit for any office or ministry.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Moreover, from the neighbouring cities we have news daily of havocs and of mortality.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Then, how Africa is being wasted by mortality and sickness I believe that you know more accurately than we do, insomuch as you are nearer to it.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But of the East those who come from thence report still more grievous desolations.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: In the midst of all these things, therefore, since you perceive that there is a general smiting as the end of the world draws near, you ought not to be too much afflicted for your own troubles.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, as becomes wise nobles, bring ye back your whole heart to the care of your souls, and fear the strict judgment all the more as it is so much nearer at hand.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Devote yourselves to piety, of which it is written that It hath promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come (1 Tim. iv.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But Almighty God is powerful both to preserve the life of your Excellency for a long time here, and to bring you after many courses of years to eternal joys.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I beg my most sweet daughters, the lady Barbara and the lady Antonina, to be greeted in my name; whom I pray that heavenly grace may protect, and grant them to be prospered in all things.
